entries.issue: Limited tool coverage and firmware gaps
entries.notes: Chinese-brand vehicle IMMO coverage in mainstream tools is updated more slowly than Japanese/Euro makes. Coverage that works today may not work after firmware updates.
entries.resolution: Always confirm specific model year in tool coverage list before quoting. Do not rely on make-level coverage claims for AKL on newer Chinese brands.

entries.issue: Battery voltage during programming
entries.notes: Smart key systems are more voltage-sensitive than blade key systems. Programming at low voltage can corrupt key data or cause partial writes.
entries.resolution: BSU mandatory on all push-start/smart key programming jobs. No exceptions.

entries.issue: Aftermarket alarm interference
entries.notes: Aftermarket alarms (Clifford, Mongoose, Python etc) frequently interfere with IMMO programming. Symptoms: key programs but intermittently fails, or programming fails entirely.
entries.resolution: Always ask about aftermarket alarms before starting. Disconnect alarm system before attempting programming. Re-enable after programming complete and tested.

entries.issue: Damaged IMMO components
entries.notes: Post-accident vehicles frequently have damaged antenna rings, BCM water ingress, or IMMO module damage that causes cryptic failures during key work.
entries.resolution: Ask about accident history. Inspect BCM/ECU connector for water/corrosion before programming. Run full diagnostic scan first.

entries.issue: Brake switch fault prevents IMMO programming sequence
entries.notes: Some IMMO programming sequences require brake pedal input. Faulty brake switch means tool cannot proceed past initial stage.
entries.resolution: Test brake lights before starting. If brake switch faulty, fix first or manually trigger brake signal if tool allows.

entries.issue: Corroded OBD port — intermittent communication during programming
entries.notes: OBD port pin corrosion is very common on vehicles used in coastal or humid environments. Causes intermittent communication drops during IMMO programming.
entries.resolution: Inspect OBD port visually before connecting. Clean with contact cleaner spray if corroded. Do not proceed with intermittent comms.

entries.issue: Programming failure from phone charger or inverter on same circuit
entries.notes: Charging a phone or running an inverter on the vehicle's 12V during key programming creates voltage noise. Causes IMMO communication errors.
entries.resolution: Disconnect all accessories from 12V during programming. BSU to battery terminals directly.

entries.issue: ID46 locked chip — cannot clone, must program via OBD
entries.notes: PCF7941 and many ID46 variants are crypto-locked. They cannot be cloned via standard clone machines. Must be programmed to vehicle IMMO via OBD.
entries.resolution: Do not attempt to clone locked ID46. Program via OBD only. If key tool says 'locked chip' — stop and use OBD method.

entries.model: Any vehicle with Texas Crypto (4D family)
entries.issue: 4D63 80-bit locked chip — requires specific tool support
entries.notes: 4D63 80-bit (used on Ford 2007+, some Mazda) requires specific chip cloning capability. Standard 4D cloners cannot clone 80-bit variants.
entries.resolution: Confirm 80-bit vs 40-bit via Key Tool Max Pro. For 80-bit, use Xhorse VVDI Key Tool with 80-bit clone token (extra cost) or program via OBD.

entries.model: Any vehicle with advanced HITAG/DST AES
entries.issue: AES/HITAG Pro transponders — cannot be cloned at all
entries.notes: AES-grade transponders (DST AES, HITAG Pro, etc.) use 128-bit encryption. Cloning is not possible. Key must be programmed to vehicle via OBD or EEPROM.
entries.resolution: Never attempt to clone AES transponders. Always use OBD programming. If OBD not supported, refer to dealer.

entries.issue: Odometer cluster reset disturbs IMMO rolling code on some platforms
entries.notes: Grey import vehicles sometimes have had odometer resets performed. This can disturb rolling codes on older IMMO systems.
entries.resolution: Be alert to signs of cluster tampering. If IMMO fails after cluster swap, perform full IMMO relearn.

entries.issue: Japanese domestic market IMMO different from AU spec
entries.notes: JDM vehicles (direct Japan imports) often have Toyota/Nissan/Honda IMMO but with JDM-specific coding. Some AU tools default to AU-spec and fail on JDM.
entries.resolution: Ask customer if vehicle is a direct Japan import. In IM608, select JDM variant if available. Confirm via VIN (JDM VIN starts differently to AU).

entries.model: A3 8V / A4 B9 / Q5 FY / Q7 4M
entries.issue: AES key system — requires online Audi server access
entries.notes: MQB Evo / MLB Evo Audi uses AES. Requires Autel IMMO active subscription AND online server. Mobile internet issues cause failures.
entries.resolution: Pre-test internet connectivity. Ensure full Autel subscription active. Not viable in areas with poor coverage.

entries.model: 3 Series E46 / 5 Series E39 / X5 E53
entries.issue: EWS3 — immobiliser key sync requires rolling code reset
entries.notes: EWS3 uses rolling codes. After battery disconnect or ECU replacement, rolling code may be out of sync. Car cranks but won't start.
entries.resolution: Perform EWS-DME sync via Autel IM608 or INPA/ISTA-D. Usually 5-minute procedure.

entries.model: 3 Series E90 / 5 Series E60 / X5 E70
entries.issue: CAS3 — 3-strike lockout during key programming
entries.notes: CAS3 module will permanently lock after 3 failed key programming attempts. Locked CAS requires replacement — $1,500+.
entries.resolution: NEVER attempt more than 2 trials. Confirm tool supports specific CAS3 variant. Ensure BSU connected. Abort if any uncertainty.

entries.model: 3 Series F30 / 5 Series F10 / X3 F25
entries.issue: FEM/BDC — requires ISN (Individual Serial Number) extraction
entries.notes: FEM/BDC IMMO requires reading the ISN from DME/ECU to sync. ISN read requires either EEPROM read of DME or specific OBD sequence on supported tools.
entries.resolution: Use Autel IM608 Pro (not standard IM608) for FEM/BDC. Required ISN read via OBD is supported on 2013+ F-series. Pre-2013 may need EEPROM.

entries.model: 5 Series G30 / 3 Series G20 / X5 G05
entries.issue: G-series — very limited aftermarket IMMO support
entries.notes: BMW G-series IMMO is largely unsupported on aftermarket tools. OEM-equivalent procedures require BMW ISTA+ dealer software.
entries.resolution: Refer G-series AKL to BMW dealer. Do not attempt with aftermarket tools — ECU corruption risk is high.

entries.issue: CAS generation mismatch
entries.notes: CAS1, CAS2, CAS3, CAS3+ all look similar externally but require different workflows. Wrong CAS version selected = failed programming or module damage.
entries.resolution: Always confirm CAS generation via IM608 or VVDI Pad Pro read-before-write. Do not guess from year alone — some late E-series had early FEM.

entries.issue: ISN mismatch after ECU swap
entries.notes: BMW ISN (Immobiliser Serial Number) must match between ECU and CAS/EWS. After ECU replacement, keys will not start car until ISN is synchronised.
entries.resolution: Confirm ISN sync is required before cutting new keys post-ECU swap. CGDI BMW or VVDI2 required for ISN read/write.

entries.issue: BSU mandatory — BMW IMMO extremely voltage-sensitive
entries.notes: BMW IMMO is the most voltage-sensitive IMMO in the market. Battery below 12.4V causes CAS/FEM fault even during normal key addition.
entries.resolution: BSU is non-negotiable on all BMW work. Check battery condition with conductance tester before starting any IMMO procedure.

entries.issue: Soldered battery in FEM module
entries.notes: FEM module has soldered internal battery that degrades over time. Dead FEM battery causes key programming failures unrelated to tool choice.
entries.resolution: If FEM programming fails despite correct tools and good car battery, suspect FEM internal battery. Specialist bench replacement required.

entries.issue: CAS3 shared with BMW E-series — same 3-strike lockout risk
entries.notes: MINI R56 uses BMW CAS3. Same lockout risk applies. Customers often surprised MINI uses BMW IMMO.
entries.resolution: Same procedure as BMW CAS3. Never exceed 2 attempts. BSU mandatory.

entries.issue: Marelli body computer IMMO — limited tool support
entries.notes: Fiat uses Marelli or Bosch body computers with varying IMMO implementations. Tool compatibility varies significantly.
entries.resolution: Check Autel compatibility carefully per year/market. Some Fiat IMMO requires Fiat-specific tools (eDia, MultiECUScan).

entries.issue: Proximity key antenna fault mimics no-start
entries.notes: Everest prox key antenna ring in door handles fails in older vehicles. IM608 shows no IMMO fault but car doesn't detect key.
entries.resolution: Test with known-good key first. Check antenna with multimeter (should be ~5 ohms). Replace antenna before blaming programming.

entries.issue: PATS module fuse — no-comms fault on programming
entries.notes: PATS module on Falcon has dedicated fuse. Blown fuse causes no communication with PATS. Often missed — tech assumes tool fault.
entries.resolution: Check PATS fuse (Fuse 29 or 30 depending on year — check fuse box lid) before connecting programmer.

entries.issue: PATS max key limit
entries.notes: Ford PATS has an 8-key maximum. Adding a 9th key will fail. System does not always give clear error — job appears to work then key does not start car.
entries.resolution: Check current key count via IM608 before cutting. If at 8, customer must decide which keys to delete first.

entries.issue: BCM security lockout
entries.notes: Excessive failed programming attempts trigger a 10-minute lockout. Rushing through errors can stack lockouts up to 30 minutes.
entries.resolution: Wait full lockout period before retrying. Do not cycle ignition rapidly. One clean attempt beats three rushed ones.

entries.model: Falcon FG X / Ford Territory SZ II
entries.issue: SecuriLock max keys reached on high-mileage fleet vehicles
entries.notes: Fleet Falcons often have 6-8 keys already programmed. SecuriLock has practical limits.
entries.resolution: Delete unused keys first. Check key count in PATS live data before adding new keys.

entries.issue: Gateway module (SGW) restriction
entries.notes: Some Next-Gen Ranger builds have SGW restricting OBD access. IM608 may not connect on first attempt.
entries.resolution: Try IM608 first. If gateway blocked, Smart Pro as backup. Document and escalate if both fail.

entries.issue: 80-bit vs 4D63 transponder confusion
entries.notes: PXII Ranger used both ID46 and 80-bit depending on build date and market. Wrong chip selection = key cut fine but no start.
entries.resolution: Always read existing key chip with Key Tool Max Pro BEFORE selecting new blank. Confirm 4D63-80-bit vs ID46.

entries.issue: Aftermarket remote interference
entries.notes: Aftermarket keyless entry modules (common on used Ranger) can block PATS programming. Symptom: key programs but intermittently fails to start.
entries.resolution: Check for aftermarket alarm/keyless modules under dash before programming. Disconnect if found.

entries.issue: PATS5 / SecuriLock advanced programming
entries.notes: PXIII uses PATS5. Next-Gen uses even more advanced system. Security access code required. Autel IM608 support is present but requires online.
entries.resolution: Confirm tool firmware up to date. Require internet access. Keep dealer contact for Next-Gen P703 AKL.

entries.issue: BCM / ECU replacement loses IMMO sync
entries.notes: After BCM or ECU replacement the IMMO sync between BCM, ECM and TCM is lost. All three modules need re-syncing.
entries.resolution: Perform module IMMO sync after any BCM/ECM replacement. Autel IM608 supports this on most models.

entries.issue: VIN mismatch on replacement BCM
entries.notes: Replacement BCMs must have VIN flashed before key programming. Aftermarket BCMs often arrive blank.
entries.resolution: Confirm BCM VIN matches vehicle before starting key work. Flash VIN first if mismatched.

entries.issue: BCM pin code required for AKL — dealer access
entries.notes: VE AKL requires BCM security code which is VIN-associated and dealer-access only without GM TIS subscription.
entries.resolution: Obtain BCM PIN via Autel IM608 IMMO calculator (supports VE) or use EEPROM read of BCM for PIN extraction.

entries.issue: Battery voltage sensitivity
entries.notes: VE BCM is extremely voltage-sensitive during programming. Drop below 12.2V and programming fails, potentially corrupting BCM key data.
entries.resolution: Mandatory BSU connection on all VE jobs. Test battery health before starting. Non-negotiable.

entries.issue: GM Global A architecture — limited aftermarket support
entries.notes: VF uses Global A platform. AKL support on aftermarket tools is limited. Autel IM608 Pro has basic support.
entries.resolution: Check Autel compatibility list before quoting VF AKL. Dealer as fallback for all-keys-lost.

entries.issue: Anti-theft lockout state
entries.notes: VF can enter anti-theft lockout if wrong key attempts exceed threshold. Lockout can be 15-60 minutes and is not always visible in fault codes.
entries.resolution: Check anti-theft status via IM608 live data first. If in lockout, wait before attempting programming.

entries.issue: TECH2 data bus required — modern OBDII tools can't access BCM
entries.notes: Older Commodore IMMO sits on Holden proprietary Class 2 data bus. Most aftermarket OBDII tools cannot communicate.
entries.resolution: Use GM TECH2 clone or Smart Pro with Holden module. Zed-Full also supports some models via dashboard cluster bypass.

entries.issue: Platform confusion
entries.notes: ZB Commodore is Opel Insignia — not GM/Holden BCM. Technicians applying VF workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm ZB requires Opel/GM European workflow. Treat as European GM, not AU Holden.

entries.issue: Dead smart key — car won't recognise for programming
entries.notes: Honda smart key must be within range AND have adequate battery. Dead key not detected at OBD level.
entries.resolution: Hold dead fob against start button. If car cranks, replace battery and use working key for AKL registration if required.

entries.model: CR-V RE / Accord CU / Jazz GE
entries.issue: AKL requires registered key present OR special EEPROM procedure
entries.notes: Honda HDSj2 immobiliser requires either a working key for AKL registration, or EEPROM read of the immobiliser ECU. Without either, AKL not possible on most tools.
entries.resolution: Quote customer: standard AKL if one key available. EEPROM-based AKL if all lost — requires ECU removal.

entries.model: Civic FC / CR-V RT / Accord CV
entries.issue: HITAG AES — specialist tool required for AKL
entries.notes: Modern Honda HITAG AES requires specific AKL procedure. Autel IM608 Pro II has partial support. Many require Honda HDS dealer access.
entries.resolution: Check Autel HITAG AES support for specific model/year. Dealer fallback required for 2020+ Honda AKL in most cases.

entries.model: Civic FD / CR-V RE / Jazz GE (ID46 era)
entries.issue: ID46 clone vs OBD decision
entries.notes: Some Honda ID46 chips from this era are locked and cannot be cloned. Attempting clone on locked chip wastes time and a chip.
entries.resolution: Always read chip type with Zed-Full first. If locked ID46 detected, switch to OBD programming immediately.

entries.issue: Import spec key profile variation
entries.notes: JDM-import HR-V occasionally has different key profiles to AU-delivered models. NSN or HON profile may vary.
entries.resolution: Always trace original key on Dolphin for import vehicles rather than cutting from code. Eliminates profile guesswork.

entries.issue: Advanced IMMO — increasing dealer-only scenarios
entries.notes: 2020+ Hyundai uses advanced encryption. Autel IM608 support exists but some models require dealer-level Hyundai GDS.
entries.resolution: Always check Autel compatibility database before quoting AKL on 2020+ Hyundai. Dealer as fallback.

entries.model: i30 PD / Tucson TL / Santa Fe TM
entries.issue: PIN required for all-keys-lost
entries.notes: AKL on 2017+ Hyundai requires 4-digit PIN from dealer or PIN extraction via OBD. Without PIN, AKL is impossible via standard tools.
entries.resolution: Obtain PIN via Autel IMMO calculator (supports some models) or customer gets dealer PIN. Quote accordingly.

entries.model: i40 YF / Veloster FS / i45 YF
entries.issue: Dead key causes no-communication fault on IMMO
entries.notes: When all key batteries are dead, IMMO module enters error state. Tool shows no communication with BCM.
entries.resolution: Hold dead fob flat against start button while attempting crank. If car starts, replace battery before reprogramming.

entries.issue: Proximity sensor pairing
entries.notes: After programming new smart key, proximity (passive entry) function occasionally does not pair correctly even when start function works.
entries.resolution: After programming: test start, lock/unlock, AND walk-away lock separately. Re-run remote programming sequence if proximity fails.

entries.issue: Immo V complexity
entries.notes: Immo V architecture significantly raises AKL risk. Some builds require Lonsdor K518 where IM608 coverage is incomplete.
entries.resolution: Always try IM608 first but have Lonsdor K518 ready. Do not quote fixed price for NX4/NQ5 AKL without confirming tool coverage first.

entries.issue: PIN code required for AKL
entries.notes: iLoad TQ AKL requires PIN code — OBD PIN read works on most but some require dealer PIN. Without PIN, all keys lost job cannot proceed.
entries.resolution: Always attempt OBD PIN read first (IM608). If PIN read fails, source from dealer before cutting any keys.

entries.model: Grand Cherokee WK2 / Cherokee KL / Wrangler JK
entries.issue: FCA IMMO — SKIM/SKREEM module programming
entries.notes: FCA platform uses SKREEM/SKIM module. AKL requires security PIN (VIN-based) and specific tool support. PIN available via Autel calculator.
entries.resolution: Use Autel IMMO PIN calculator for FCA. Autel IM608 supports most WK2/KL. Confirm year before quoting.

entries.issue: SGW trim variability
entries.notes: Not all JL Wranglers have SGW — depends on spec level and build date. Cannot confirm without testing.
entries.resolution: Always test IM608 first on JL. If SGW present, escalate. If not, proceed normally.

entries.issue: Advanced FCA IMMO — dealer SCN required for AKL
entries.notes: JL Wrangler uses FCA 5th gen IMMO. AKL not reliably possible with current aftermarket tools.
entries.resolution: Refer JL Wrangler AKL to Jeep dealer. Key additions with one working key supported on Autel.

entries.model: Grand Cherokee WK2 / Cherokee KL / Compass MP
entries.issue: SGW (Secure Gateway) blocking
entries.notes: FCA SGW module prevents OBD access for key programming on later builds. IM608 may connect to diagnostics but IMMO functions are blocked.
entries.resolution: Always test for SGW presence before quoting AKL. If blocked, Abrites or specialist required. Quote as high-risk with possible escalation surcharge.

entries.model: Sportage QL / Cerato BD / Rio YB
entries.issue: Proximity key antenna failure — intermittent no-start
entries.notes: Kia prox key antenna in B-pillar fails in this generation. IM608 shows no fault but key not detected intermittently.
entries.resolution: Test with substitute key. Check antenna harness in B-pillar. Replace antenna before re-programming.

entries.model: Stinger CK / Sorento MQ4 / Carnival KA4
entries.issue: PIN required — shared with Hyundai platform
entries.notes: Same PIN requirement as Hyundai on shared platform. AKL without PIN not possible on standard tools.
entries.resolution: Obtain PIN via Autel calculator or dealer. Advise customer PIN cost is additional.

entries.issue: BCM and PATS sync after any module replacement
entries.notes: Disco 3/4 has multiple modules that share IMMO data. BCM, CJB, and ECM must all be in sync. Replacing any one without sync leaves car immobilised.
entries.resolution: Perform IMMO relearn sequence after any module change. Autel IM608 or JLR-specific SDD required.

entries.issue: BECM and BJB module dependency
entries.notes: L322 and Discovery 3/4 have multiple body modules (BECM, BJB) that all need to be in correct state for key programming. Module faults cause cryptic failures.
entries.resolution: Run full diagnostic scan before any key work. Resolve body module faults first. Do not attempt key programming on vehicles with multiple BECM/BJB DTCs.

entries.model: Range Rover L405 / Discovery 5 / Defender L663
entries.issue: JLR Pivi/SPA architecture
entries.notes: Modern JLR SPA/Pivi platform AKL is extremely high risk without Abrites. Partial programming attempts can lock out vehicle permanently.
entries.resolution: Never attempt AKL on L405/L462/L663 without Abrites or confirmed JLR specialist tool. Quote as refer-out or specialist surcharge job.

entries.issue: Advanced JLR IMMO — Autel support limited on late models
entries.notes: L405/L462 late models use advanced JLR IMMO. AKL on 2018+ L405 and all L462 very challenging with aftermarket tools.
entries.resolution: Use JLR-specific tools (Topdon, AVDI with JLR license) for best results. Refer complex AKL to JLR dealer.

entries.issue: Isuzu D-Max platform
entries.notes: New BT-50 TF is based on Isuzu D-Max — not Mazda. Applying Mazda workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm BT-50 TF requires Isuzu D-Max workflow on IM608. Quote and treat as Isuzu job.

entries.issue: Immo III smart key clone attempt
entries.notes: PCF7941/7953 chips on Immo III cannot be cloned. Customers sometimes request clone — impossible on these.
entries.resolution: Advise customer upfront: Immo III smart keys require OBD programming — no clone option. Quote accordingly.

entries.issue: Smart key antenna sensitivity — aftermarket key range issues
entries.notes: MX-5 ND has a small fob antenna. Aftermarket smart keys often have significantly reduced range vs OEM.
entries.resolution: Advise customer of potential range difference with aftermarket key. OEM-quality Xhorse/Autel keys perform better.

entries.model: Mazda CX-5 KF / Mazda3 BP / CX-9 TC
entries.issue: Mazda SKE AES — BSU mandatory
entries.notes: SKE AES era Mazda will corrupt IMMO if voltage drops during programming. Results in all keys lost requiring dealer EEPROM fix.
entries.resolution: Mandatory BSU connection. Voltage below 12.6V should abort job. Do not proceed on questionable batteries.

entries.model: Mazda3 BK / BL / Mazda6 GG / GH
entries.issue: Max 3 keys on older SKE IMMO
entries.notes: Older Mazda SKE allows maximum 3 keys. 4th key silently fails or causes existing key deregistration.
entries.resolution: Check key count before adding. Advise customer on 3-key limit. Erase unused keys if needed.

entries.issue: Online authentication required for AES key programming
entries.notes: Some late Mazda AES platforms require Autel online server authentication. Offline programming fails.
entries.resolution: Ensure Autel subscription active and site has mobile internet. Pre-test connectivity before attending.

entries.issue: EIS replacement procedure
entries.notes: Replacing EIS module on these vehicles requires specific synchronisation sequence with ECU and gearbox module. Simply swapping EIS = non-start.
entries.resolution: Always advise customer of EIS sync requirement before quoting module replacement jobs. CGDI MB or Abrites required.

entries.model: C-Class W203 / E-Class W211 / Vito W639
entries.issue: EZS infrared key — standard OBD tools cannot program
entries.notes: EZS-equipped Mercs use infrared key communication, not transponder. Most aftermarket tools cannot program EZS keys without specialist hardware.
entries.resolution: Only attempt with AVDI (Abrites) or Mercedes XENTRY. Standard Autel IM608 cannot program EZS via OBD. Quote accordingly.

entries.model: C-Class W204 / E-Class W212 / GLK X204
entries.issue: EIS counter — failed attempts lock the EIS
entries.notes: EIS allows limited failed authentication attempts before permanent lockout. Locked EIS requires replacement and VIN-coding — expensive.
entries.resolution: BSU mandatory. Use only supported tools (Autel IM608 Pro supports W204/W212 via OBD). Do not exceed 2 attempts.

entries.model: C-Class W205 / E-Class W213 / GLC X253
entries.issue: Advanced IMMO — requires Mercedes online server
entries.notes: W205+ requires online Mercedes SCN coding for key programming. Autel IM608 Pro II has some support but success variable.
entries.resolution: Recommend Abrites AVDI for reliable W205+ key work. Autel as backup for some models. Dealer for complex AKL.

entries.issue: SGW gateway restriction
entries.notes: Latest Sprinter has SGW module restricting OBD access. IM608 may connect but AKL functions blocked.
entries.resolution: Test IM608 first. If SGW blocks, escalate to specialist. Do not force — partial writes on VS30 can be expensive.

entries.issue: SCN coding required after BCM replacement
entries.notes: Replacement BCM must be SCN-coded to vehicle via Mercedes XENTRY. Uncoded BCM causes IMMO mismatch.
entries.resolution: Source used/coded BCM where possible. New BCM requires dealer XENTRY SCN coding. Cannot be done with standard aftermarket tools.

entries.issue: Steering column lock (ESL) coupling
entries.notes: KESSY-era Mercedes have electronic steering lock (ESL) that must be functioning for start. Failed ESL prevents start even with correct key.
entries.resolution: Diagnose ESL fault separately from IMMO fault. ESL failure is common on W204/W205. Advise customer — ESL repair is separate job.

entries.issue: Hybrid system interference
entries.notes: PHEV high-voltage system can interfere with OBD programming. Some builds require hybrid system to be in specific state before IMMO access.
entries.resolution: Follow Mitsubishi PHEV-specific procedure: ensure vehicle is in READY mode off before connecting. Do not attempt with hybrid system active.

entries.issue: PIN required for AKL on advanced IMMO
entries.notes: 2018+ Mitsubishi advanced system requires dealer PIN for AKL. Autel IM608 support limited.
entries.resolution: Obtain dealer PIN. Advise customer this is additional cost. Quote accordingly.

entries.issue: IMMO system relearn after battery disconnect
entries.notes: After main battery disconnect, older Pajero IMMO occasionally requires key relearn. Usually self-recovers on next start.
entries.resolution: Insert key and leave in ON position for 30 seconds. Then attempt to start. If still immobilised, use IMMO reset via tool.

entries.issue: Key count verification
entries.notes: Pajero NS+ can hold up to 4 keys. Reaching limit causes programming to fail silently.
entries.resolution: Always check key count before programming. Erase old keys if at limit.

entries.issue: Smart key system — antenna failure in door mirrors
entries.notes: Triton MQ/MR prox antenna in door mirrors fails. Key detected only close to antenna location.
entries.resolution: Test with known-good key. Check antenna connectors in door mirrors. Common in vehicles exposed to water ingress.

entries.model: Navara D22 / X-Trail T30 / Pulsar N16
entries.issue: NATS 4 — 4-attempt lockout on failed programming
entries.notes: NATS 4 IMMO locks after 4 consecutive failed key programming attempts. Lock requires dealer Consult II reset.
entries.resolution: Do not make more than 2-3 attempts. Use correct tool and procedure. Reset via Consult II before third attempt if uncertain.

entries.issue: Smart key blade compatibility
entries.notes: Some D23 builds use different blade profiles across model years. NSN14 works on most but confirm before cutting.
entries.resolution: Verify blade profile by decoding original key before cutting replacement. Trace on Dolphin if existing key available.

entries.issue: NATS 5 — BCM and ECM sync required after AKL
entries.notes: AKL on D40 requires IMMO sync between BCM and ECM after key programming. Skipping sync step leaves car immobilised.
entries.resolution: Perform BCM-ECM IMMO sync after key registration. Autel IM608 prompts for this automatically.

entries.issue: NATS antenna ring wear
entries.notes: High-mileage GU Patrols frequently have worn antenna rings causing intermittent no-start. Often misdiagnosed as transponder or ECU fault.
entries.resolution: Always test antenna ring resistance before programming. 3-6 ohm is normal. Open circuit = ring failure, not chip fault.

entries.issue: NATS V AKL risk
entries.notes: Y62 Patrol AKL is high-value and high-risk. NATS V requires careful tool selection. Failure mid-sequence can lock out system.
entries.resolution: Always run IM608 first on Y62. Have Lonsdor K518 staged and ready. Do not attempt without BSU connected. Quote as high-risk job.

entries.model: Patrol Y62 / Murano Z52 / Pathfinder R52
entries.issue: AES key programming requires specific Nissan PIN
entries.notes: AES era Nissan AKL requires security PIN. Autel IM608 Pro II has calculator support for some. Others require dealer.
entries.resolution: Check Autel compatibility. If not supported, quote customer dealer AKL. Obtain BCM part number first.

entries.issue: Max 8 keys — fleet vehicles often at limit
entries.notes: NATS allows up to 8 registered keys. Fleet/rental vehicles may be at limit, causing silent programming failure.
entries.resolution: Check key count before adding. Delete deregistered keys first if at limit.

entries.issue: BSI coding required after key programming
entries.notes: Peugeot BSI (Body Systems Interface) must be recoded after key addition. Skipping coding step leaves BSI out of sync.
entries.resolution: Always perform BSI coding after key programming. Autel IM608 prompts for this in standard flow.

entries.issue: BSI programming requires online PSA server access
entries.notes: Modern PSA Group (Peugeot/Citroen/DS) requires online PSA server for some key operations. Offline programming not possible.
entries.resolution: Ensure internet access. Check Autel PSA online compatibility for model/year before quoting.

entries.issue: VAG vs Porsche workflow
entries.notes: Cayenne 9PA shares VAG platform but Porsche-specific IMMO adds complexity beyond standard VAG workflow.
entries.resolution: Use VVDI Pad Pro as primary (VAG strength) but be prepared to escalate to Abrites if standard VAG workflow fails.

entries.model: Megane II / Scenic II / Laguna II
entries.issue: UCH immobiliser PIN required for AKL
entries.notes: Renault UCH (Unite Centrale Habitacle) stores PIN. AKL requires PIN extraction via EEPROM read of UCH module.
entries.resolution: EEPROM read of UCH for PIN. Autel IM608 supports PIN calculation for some models. Physical UCH removal may be needed.

entries.model: Megane III / Scenic III / Clio IV
entries.issue: Hands-free card — antenna and antenna ring failures
entries.notes: Renault hands-free card system relies on antenna ring under dashboard. Ring failures cause intermittent no-start or no-detection.
entries.resolution: Test antenna ring resistance. Should be ~10-15 ohms. Common failure point on 100,000+ km vehicles.

entries.issue: Shared Toyota/Subaru platform — use Toyota IMMO procedure
entries.notes: BRZ and 86 use the same IMMO. Must use Toyota 86 / Subaru BRZ specific procedure, not generic Subaru or Toyota flow.
entries.resolution: Select specifically 'BRZ/86' in IM608 menu. Generic Subaru Forester procedure will fail.

entries.model: Forester SJ / Outback BS / Liberty BS
entries.issue: Key learn procedure requires specific button sequence
entries.notes: Subaru key learning requires vehicle-specific button press sequence in addition to OBD programming. Skipping causes learn failure.
entries.resolution: Follow Autel IM608 on-screen prompts carefully. The button sequence step is easy to miss in the workflow.

entries.model: Forester SK / Outback BT (smart key era)
entries.issue: Push-start proximity pairing
entries.notes: After programming new smart key, passive entry function occasionally requires a second pairing cycle.
entries.resolution: Standard fix: complete programming sequence, then run remote learning cycle via IM608. Test all smart key functions before leaving.

entries.issue: Performance tune ECU incompatibility
entries.notes: Tuned/modified WRX/STI frequently have aftermarket ECUs or piggyback tunes that break standard IMMO workflow.
entries.resolution: Always ask customer if vehicle is tuned before starting. Tuned ECUs often require reverting to stock ECU for key work. Advise accordingly.

entries.model: WRX VA / Forester SK / Outback BT (2018+)
entries.issue: Advanced IMMO — dealer PIN required in some AKL scenarios
entries.notes: Newer Subaru IMMO requires dealer security PIN for AKL. Autel IM608 Pro II has some support but not all year/model combinations.
entries.resolution: Check IM608 compatibility. For unsupported models, refer to Subaru dealer or obtain PIN via Subaru AU.

entries.issue: Smart key antenna fault
entries.notes: DST80 smart key antenna ring can fail on high-mileage vehicles. Symptoms: intermittent no-start, key not detected. IM608 may show no IMMO fault.
entries.resolution: Check antenna ring resistance (should be 3-6 ohms) with multimeter before blaming key. Replace ring first — cheap fix vs ECU.

entries.model: Camry XV70 / Corolla E210 / RAV4 XA50
entries.issue: AES key requires online activation
entries.notes: DST AES generation requires Autel online account with active subscription to program. Offline programming not possible.
entries.resolution: Ensure Autel account active and internet available before attending job. Check MaxiIM subscription status.

entries.issue: Key learns but won't start in cold weather
entries.notes: Rare but documented: DST80 key programmed in shop starts fine but intermittently fails to start in cold (under 10°C) conditions.
entries.resolution: Re-learn key with BSU connected, ensuring stable voltage throughout. Test cold start before handing over.

entries.issue: BMW FEM platform confusion
entries.notes: GR Supra uses BMW FEM architecture — not Toyota IMMO. Attempting Toyota workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm Supra A90 requires BMW FEM workflow (VVDI Pad Pro). Quote as BMW-level job.

entries.issue: Dealer-required AKL on Australian spec
entries.notes: H300 AU spec AKL requires dealer Toyota Techstream and specific immobiliser unlock procedure. Not supported on most aftermarket tools as of 2024.
entries.resolution: Quote customer dealer AKL via Toyota dealer. Do not attempt with standard tools — ECU lock risk.

entries.issue: Fake/cloned transponder failures
entries.notes: Cheap eBay/AliExpress cloned keys often fail to start even when programmed. Chip reads as valid but signal strength too low.
entries.resolution: Use quality OEM-equivalent blanks (Silca, JMA, Xhorse). Avoid cheap Chinese clones for Toyota G and 4D chips.

entries.issue: Key count limit
entries.notes: Maximum 5 keys on DST80 smart system. If 5 keys already registered, programming 6th will fail silently or error.
entries.resolution: Check key count via IM608 live data before starting. Advise customer to erase old/lost keys if at limit.

entries.issue: Key count limit — max 5 keys on DST80
entries.notes: Maximum 5 keys on DST80 smart system. Programming 6th will fail silently or error.
entries.resolution: Check key count via IM608 live data before starting. Advise customer to erase old/lost keys if at limit.

entries.model: Hilux N70 / HiAce H200 / Corolla E140
entries.issue: Chip mismatch
entries.notes: 4D67 vs G-chip confusion on same platform. Vehicles look identical but require different chip. Wrong chip = key cuts fine but vehicle will not start.
entries.resolution: Always read existing chip with Zed-Full or Key Tool Max Pro BEFORE cutting. Confirm 4D67 vs G-chip before ordering blank.

entries.issue: Low battery programming failure
entries.notes: Programming fails or key count corrupts if battery below 12.4V during DST80 programming. Can result in all keys locked out.
entries.resolution: ALWAYS connect battery support unit (BSU) before any programming on 200 Series. Check battery health first.

entries.issue: Advanced key system — very limited aftermarket support
entries.notes: 300 Series uses advanced AES with added vehicle security. Autel IM608 Pro II has partial support but success rates variable.
entries.resolution: Advise customer of risk. Test with DEMO key before committing to full AKL. Keep Toyota dealer as fallback.

entries.issue: Hybrid IMMO — 12V battery weakness during programming
entries.notes: Prius 12V auxiliary battery is tiny and degrades quickly. Programming during low 12V causes IMU faults.
entries.resolution: Connect BSU to 12V aux battery terminal (not main HV). Never attempt programming with HV battery only.

entries.issue: KESSY module software mismatch after coding
entries.notes: Coding wrong SW version to KESSY module causes IMMO mismatch. Car may start initially then fail after BCM learns new code.
entries.resolution: Always match KESSY software version to VIN using ETKA/Elsawin before coding. Autel IM608 auto-selects in most cases.

entries.issue: Commercial variant IMMO differences from passenger car
entries.notes: Amarok uses different IMMO variant than Golf/Passat despite similar platform. Crafter 2011-2016 uses Mercedes Sprinter IMMO.
entries.resolution: Select Amarok/Crafter specifically in IM608. Do not use standard Golf procedure. Crafter 2016+ uses VW standard.

entries.issue: ID48 Crypto clone complexity
entries.notes: ID48 (PCF7941) requires crypto key calculation for cloning. Zed-Full handles most but some builds have locked chips.
entries.resolution: Attempt Zed-Full clone first. If locked chip detected, switch to OBD programming workflow with VVDI Pad Pro.

entries.issue: MQB Evo PIN extraction
entries.notes: VAG MQB Evo PIN extraction requires VVDI Pad Pro or Abrites — IM608 alone often cannot extract PIN on these platforms.
entries.resolution: Always use VVDI Pad Pro as primary on MQB Evo VAG. Have Abrites contact ready for escalation. Do not commit to fixed price without confirming PIN read capability.

entries.issue: IMMO 4 — security access required before key programming
entries.notes: VAG IMMO 4 requires security access (login) before key addition. Many technicians skip this step — programming appears to succeed but key won't start car.
entries.resolution: Always perform security access (adaptation channel 21 or specific login via VCDS/Autel) before adding keys.

entries.model: Golf VII / Passat B8 / Tiguan AD / Polo AW (MQB)
entries.issue: MQB security access — component protection
entries.notes: MQB platform has component protection on BCM/KESSY. After coding or module replacement, component protection must be released via VW online system.
entries.resolution: Use Autel IM608 Component Protection function with active MaxiIM subscription. Requires internet. Cannot be done offline.

entries.issue: Wrong tool choice = ECU damage risk
entries.notes: Attempting VAG Immo 5 work with IM608 alone (without VVDI or Abrites) risks partial writes that can corrupt ECU/IMMO module.
entries.resolution: VVDI Pad Pro is mandatory primary for all VAG Immo 5+ work. Never attempt full AKL on VAG with IM608 alone.

entries.model: XC60 second gen / XC90 second gen / S60 third gen (SPA)
entries.issue: SPA platform AKL risk
entries.notes: Volvo SPA AKL without Abrites is extremely high risk. Partial key programming can lock vehicle permanently.
entries.resolution: Treat all SPA Volvo AKL as refer-out unless Abrites is available. Quote accordingly — do not attempt with IM608 alone.
